articles.title: Leading Economic And Employment Indicators Are Warnings To Investors
articles.summary: With risk assets at historic valuations, investors must remain nimble as leading indicators suggest smaller payroll gains and heightened downside risk.

articles.title: FTSE 100 live: Rate cut seen as jobs market weakens, oil below $60
articles.summary: The rate of unemployment has risen to its highest level in almost a decade outside the pandemic as employers continue to shed jobs. The increase to 5.1% makes it even more likely that the Bank of England will order an interest rate cut on Thursday.

articles.title: I Don't Trust The Current Unemployment Rate
articles.summary: Unemployment looks steady, but part-time work and falling full-time jobs signal fragility. Click here to read what investors need to know.

articles.title: Chicago Fed Puts October Unemployment Rate at 4.35%, Little Changed From Last Official Report
articles.summary: WASHINGTON (Reuters) -The U.S. unemployment rate remained stable at around 4.3% in October according to a Chicago Federal Reserve bank economic model that uses private data to estimate the jobless rate even in the absence of government statistics that have ...

articles.title: US Payrolls Rise After October Drop, Unemployment Marches Higher
articles.summary: US job growth remained sluggish in November and the unemployment rate rose to a four-year high, pointing to a continued cooling in the labor market after a weak October. Nonfarm payrolls increased 64,000 in November after declining 105,000 in October ...
